sections.paragraph:
Δημήτριος, ου, ὁ (occurs freq.: SIG, OGI ind.; LXX; Demetr.: 722 Jac.; Joseph.) Demetrius

① an otherwise unknown Christian 3J 12.

② a silversmith in Ephesus, leader of a demonstration against Paul Ac 19:24, 38. JChapman, JTS 5, 1904, 364ff identifies 1 w. Δημᾶς; VBartlet, ibid. 6, 1905, 208f, 215 identifies the two Demtr. of the NT. See ABludau, D. Aufstand d. Silberschmieds Dem. Ac 19:23–40: Der Katholik 86, 1906, 81–92; 201–13; 258–72; Haenchen ad loc.; also reff. in SEG XXXVI, 1028; XLII, 1029.—LGPN I. M-M.

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

ind. ind. = index

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

JTS JTS = Journal of Theological Studies—List 6

ibid. ibid. = ibidem (in the same book or passage)

NT NT = New Testament

Haenchen Haenchen = EH., Commentary on Acts—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

LGPN LGPN = A Lexicon of Greek Personal Names—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 222
